title: OPSS Invites Researchers: New Areas of Research Interest

content.text: Are you passionate about advancing product safety and driving innovation? At the Office for Product Safety and Standards (OPSS), we are excited to introduce our Areas of Research Interest (ARIs): an open call to academic and industry researchers to engage with the questions that will shape the future of UK product safety and regulation.

As the UK’s product regulator, we protect people and places from product-related harm by working across government, with local authorities, public sector organisations, businesses, and business and consumer bodies to fulfil our responsibilities. The Department of Business and Trade (DBT), which OPSS is part of, have published ARIs featuring OPSS. OPSS has also produced specific ARIs which supports DBTs agenda for stronger economic growth. They focus on the key questions we should answer to ensure products are safe, reliable and foster confidence among consumers and businesses.
Our work is grounded in robust evidence, science and strategic intelligence, to streamline regulation, reduce complexity and support a thriving market, enabling trade and innovation.

We aim to improve the evidence base through our Strategic Research Programme, Registers of Specialists and our flagship Research Fellowship Scheme. By publishing our ARIs, we spotlight the research questions that matter most to our policymakers and regulators, and we invite you to help us find the answers.

This is a launchpad for collaboration and discovery. It’s designed to spark opportunities for future partnerships across academia, industry and government, drawing on expertise to inform effective policy to safeguard consumers and champion businesses across the UK.
Why does research matter for OPSS?

The importance of aligning government research needs with the wider research community was highlighted in the 2015 Nurse Review, leading to the creation of government ARIs. These foster collaboration, address real-world challenges, ignite innovative partnerships, and deliver better value for public investment through lasting impact. Current ARIs are searchable in the ARI database.
OPSS has identified key research questions essential for evidence-based policy and consumer safety, organised under five strategic priority themes.
If your research addresses any of these themes or your skills could support, we want to hear from you!
Why get involved? By contributing your expertise, you can:

OPSS is planning to host a launch event to provide insights into our research priorities, foster collaboration, and strengthen our research networks. If you are involved in relevant research and are interested in attending, please contact: OPSS.Research@businessandtrade.gov.uk
You can also offer specialist advice to OPSS by applying to join our ‘Register of Specialists’.
Delve into our Areas of Research Interest, connect with policymakers and work together to ensure the UK’s product safety system is informed by the strongest possible evidence.
